family: Fries-, Photo-Fries, and Anionic Ortho-Fries Rearrangement
rationale: The Fries rearrangement of phenyl acetate is the quintessential textbook example. It demonstrates the Lewis acid-catalyzed acyl migration from a phenolic oxygen to the ortho and para positions of the aromatic ring, yielding a mixture of 2-hydroxyacetophenone and 4-hydroxyacetophenone. This example is simple, uses common functional groups, and clearly illustrates the core transformation of the reaction family.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses a simple phenyl acetate substrate, avoiding complex polycyclic or substituted arenes that could overlap with other named reactions involving aromatic systems. The transformation is a specific acyl migration, distinct from Claisen rearrangements (allyl migration), benzidine rearrangements (diarylamine rearrangement), or Friedel-Crafts acylations (which typically involve a separate acylating agent and aromatic substrate).
